changed_construction_audit.plain_language_claim: Cantonese attests preverbal 未 and 冇 before an experiential V過 predicate. Final 未 questions, 有冇 questions, non-experiential 未V, and general 唔 or prohibitive 咪 negation remain separate.

sources.citation: Lam, Cherry Chit-Yu. 2018. On the Interaction between Negation and Aspect in Grammaticalisation.
Matthews, Stephen and Virginia Yip. 2011. Cantonese: A Comprehensive Grammar, multimedia supplement, Chapter 11.
sources.locator: 11.2.5 experiential 過 and final 未; 11.2.6 冇 auxiliary

sources.citation: Zhang, Hongnian 張洪年. 1970. 粵語中常見的謂詞詞尾.
鄭定歐、張勵妍、高石英. 2021. 粵語（香港話）教程（修訂版）.

unresolved_questions: Which verbs, objects, particles, and discourse environments productively license preverbal 未/冇 + V過?
How should longer object NPs be grouped internally without broadening the negative-experiential claim?
What corpus-frequency and role-neutral panel evidence supports the checked profiles?

implementation_validation: At v0.5.211, regression remains 551/551, NP remains 43/43, and 1,246 construction assertions across 164 active labels pass. Three exact textbook statements produce NegativeExperiential; five non-target negator, aspect, and question profiles do not.
linguistic_confidence: The status is research_pending, not provisional or supported. Checked sources establish a narrow preverbal 未/冇 + V過 statement family, while zero usable panel judgments and an incomplete productive boundary inventory block promotion.
overclaim_review_scope: Reviewed the NegativeExperiential template, the experiential compatibility fallback, the directional-negation template that previously absorbed 未去, both zero-weight implementation probes, three exact source positives, and five explicit absence boundaries.
unresolved_release_questions: The remaining 53 implementation-only labels still require source/runtime reconciliation.
The full retirement-review window remains open and must be completed no later than sequence 41.
RUL and PFV survey integration remains paused pending clean locked-instrument evidence.
